[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 益智录 - Title meaning 'Records of Enriching Wisdom' or 'Notes on Cultivating Intelligence.'
  • 冤缢 - Compound word meaning 'wrongful hanging' or 'unjust strangulation.'
  • 散花天女 - Literally 'Scattering Flower Heavenly Maiden,' a reference to a Buddhist deity often associated with the scattering of flowers as a symbol of enlightenment or grace.
  • 义犬 - Literally 'righteous dog,' a term often used to describe a loyal or heroic dog in Chinese folklore.
  • 节烈女 - Refers to a chaste and loyal woman, often used in historical or literary contexts to describe women who remained faithful to their husbands or families.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《益智录》 - A collection of stories or anecdotes, likely intended to impart wisdom or moral lessons. The title suggests that the text is meant to be educational or instructive.
  • 鬼联 - Likely refers to a story involving ghosts or supernatural beings, possibly in the form of a chain of events or a series of interconnected ghostly incidents.
  • 狐夫人 - Refers to a fox spirit or fox wife, a common motif in Chinese folklore where foxes are often depicted as shape-shifters or supernatural beings who take on human form.
  • 散花天女 - A Buddhist concept, often associated with the Mahayana tradition, where the Scattering Flower Heavenly Maiden is a deity who scatters flowers as a symbol of enlightenment or spiritual purity.
  • 义犬 - A common theme in Chinese folklore, where dogs are depicted as loyal, brave, or heroic, often in stories that emphasize moral lessons.

Proper Nouns

  • 《益智录》 - Title of the text, should be consistently rendered as 'Records of Enriching Wisdom' or 'Notes on Cultivating Intelligence.'
  • 鬼联 - Should be consistently rendered as 'Ghost Chain' or 'Chain of Ghosts' to convey the idea of interconnected ghostly events.
  • 狐夫人 - Should be consistently rendered as 'Fox Lady' or 'Fox Spirit' to convey the supernatural nature of the character.
  • 散花天女 - Should be consistently rendered as 'Scattering Flower Heavenly Maiden' to maintain the Buddhist connotation.
  • 义犬 - Should be consistently rendered as 'Righteous Dog' or 'Loyal Dog' to convey the moral or heroic qualities of the character.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人狐换亲 - Literal phrase meaning 'human and fox exchange marriage,' referring to a supernatural or folkloric event where a human and a fox spirit exchange spouses.
  • 请乩 - Practice involving divination or communication with spirits through a medium, often involving a '乩' (a tool used in such rituals).
  • 来生债 - Concept referring to debts or karmic ties carried over from past lives.
  • 旱脚鱼 - Term that could refer to a type of fish or a metaphorical expression, but its exact meaning is unclear without further context.
  • 恶梦 - Literally 'bad dream,' often used to describe nightmares or ominous dreams.
  • 陇州三案 - Refers to three cases or events related to the region of Longzhou (陇州), possibly involving legal or supernatural matters.
  • 虚娘 - Name that appears in the text, likely referring to a character or figure in the story.
  • 矫娘 - Name that appears in the text, likely referring to a character or figure in the story.
  • 碧玉 - Name that appears in the text, likely referring to a character or figure in the story.
  • 阿娇 - Name that appears in the text, likely referring to a character or figure in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 李智 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 伍丽娘 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 谷一穗 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.

  • 美人图 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 某邑案 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 瑞雪 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 赵诚 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 许翠娥 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 齐氏 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 苏成 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 颜星丽 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 太原娼 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.
  • 贺梦龄 - Name of an individual or character that appears in the text.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卷之十 - Refers to 'Volume Ten,' a common way to organize texts in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 卷之十一 - Refers to 'Volume Eleven,' continuing the organizational structure of the text.
  • 附录一：序、跋、题辞 - Refers to 'Appendix One: Preface, Postscript, Inscription,' which are common components of traditional Chinese texts.

Proper Nouns

  • 耿如梅 - Transliterated as 'Geng Rumei.'
  • 林芳 - Transliterated as 'Lin Fang.'
  • 李司训 - Transliterated as 'Li Sishun.'
  • 蒋廉 - Transliterated as 'Jiang Lian.'
  • 余母 - Transliterated as 'Yu Mu.'
  • 严三凤 - Transliterated as 'Yan Sanfeng.'
  • 董二晕 - Transliterated as 'Dong Er Yun.'
  • 绛云 - Transliterated as 'Jiang Yun.'
  • 查修文 - Transliterated as 'Zha Xiwen.'
  • 何福 - Transliterated as 'He Fu.'

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何方欲再言 - Formal expression meaning 'How Fang (the name) was about to speak again.'
  • 胡曰 - 'Hu said,' where '胡' is a proper name.
  • 君何姓、福名、字德有，辛酉科之拔贡乎？ - Formal question asking, 'Are you surnamed He, given name Fu, courtesy name De You, and a selected scholar from the Xin You (1891) examination?'
  • 先生何以知之？ - 'How did you know this?' where '先生' is a respectful address.
  • 君年庚三十有二，新春甲寅寅时生乎？ - 'Are you thirty-two years old, born on the first day of the New Year in the Jia Yin (甲寅) year at the hour of Yin?'
  • 不胜惊异 - 'Could not help but be astonished.'
  • 愿闻先生先知之故 - 'I wish to hear the reason for your prior knowledge.'
  • 蒙仙人指示 - 'I was shown by a divine being.'
  • 仆年届古稀 - 'I have reached the age of seventy.'
  • 只有一女 - 'I have only one daughter.'
  • 及笄未字 - 'She is of marriageable age but unmarried.'
  • 家有糟糠 - 'I have a wife who is content with simple fare.'
  • 相容则同居，不合则各爨 - 'If we can tolerate each other, we will live together; if not, we will live separately.'
  • 实与君大有利益 - 'It will actually be very beneficial for you.'
  • 今日良辰 - 'Today is a good time.'
  • 非娶嫡 - 'It is not a formal marriage.'
  • 鼓吹灯彩，概可从略 - 'The ceremonial music and decorations can be omitted.'
  • 惟冠带行礼可耳 - 'Only the formal attire and ceremony are necessary.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 拔贡 - A type of imperial examination that selected scholars for official positions.
  • 辛酉科 - Refers to the Xin You (1891) year, indicating the year of the examination.
  • 古稀 - A traditional Chinese term for the age of seventy, derived from a poem by Du Fu.
  • 及笄 - The age of fifteen or sixteen, when a girl is considered ready for marriage.
  • 糟糠 - Refers to a wife who is content with simple fare, often used to describe a humble or plain wife.
  • 冠带行礼 - Refers to the formal attire and ceremony of a traditional Chinese wedding.

Proper Nouns

  • 何方 - A proper name, likely 'Fang.'
  • 胡 - A proper name, likely 'Hu.'
  • 德有 - A courtesy name, 'De You.'
  • 仙人 - A divine being or sage.
  • 古稀 - A term for the age of seventy.
  • 及笄 - A term for the age of fifteen or sixteen.
  • 糟糠 - A term for a wife who is content with simple fare.
  • 冠带行礼 - A term for the formal attire and ceremony of a traditional Chinese wedding.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 四品顶带 - Refers to the insignia of a fourth-rank official, symbolizing a certain level of social status and authority.
  • 功名卑贱 - Literally means 'lowly in official rank and reputation,' indicating a modest or humble social standing.
  • 僭分 - Means 'to overstep one's bounds' or 'to exceed one's proper status,' suggesting an inappropriate elevation in social or official rank.
  • 故物 - Literally means 'old thing' or 'former possession,' indicating something belonging to the past or previously owned.
  • 衣帽猝不及备 - Means 'not having time to prepare proper attire,' indicating a sudden need for clothing.
  • 服之行礼 - Means 'to wear it and perform the ceremony,' indicating the act of wearing the clothing for a specific ritual.
  • 礼毕脱去 - Means 'to remove it after the ceremony,' indicating the removal of the clothing once the ritual is completed.
  • 绝代佳姝 - Literally means 'a woman of unparalleled beauty,' indicating an exceptionally beautiful woman.
  • 德有之，器小哉 - A phrase that can be interpreted as 'virtue has it, but the vessel is small,' suggesting that while the person has virtue, their capacity or potential is limited.
  • 心实疑焉 - Means 'my heart truly doubts this,' indicating a genuine sense of skepticism or disbelief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 四品顶带 - Refers to the insignia of a fourth-rank official in the imperial bureaucracy, which would have been a significant symbol of status.
  • 功名卑贱 - Reflects the hierarchical nature of the imperial examination system and the social stratification based on official rank.
  • 僭分 - Highlights the importance of social hierarchy and the consequences of overstepping one's place in society.
  • 绝代佳姝 - Describes a woman of extraordinary beauty, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 德有之，器小哉 - A phrase that reflects Confucian values of virtue and the balance between moral excellence and practical ability.

Proper Nouns

  • 四品顶带 - The insignia of a fourth-rank official.
  • 小生 - A modest term for oneself, often used by scholars or officials.
  • 胡 - The host or a person of higher social status.
  • 老夫 - An older man or a person of higher social status.
  • 女 - The bride.
  • 天地 - Heaven and Earth, often worshipped in traditional Chinese ceremonies.
  • 女父母 - The bride's parents.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以卿门第而婚于贫生 - Marriage of your noble family to a poor scholar.
  • 以卿美貌而甘居媵妾 - Your beauty and willingness to be a concubine.
  • 与卿父素不识面，而吾之姓字年庚历言不爽 - Despite never having met your father, my name, age, and other details are all correct.
  • 温柔有乡，流连不觉旬日 - In the warmth and comfort, we stayed for ten days without realizing it.
  • 破镜飞天，妾自至，勿悬望也 - The broken mirror flies to the sky, I will come on my own, do not wait anxiously.
  • 盍偕归乎 - Why don't you come back with me?
  • 得无封粮之由单乎 - Is it not the receipt for the grain tax?
  • 昨已投柜完纳矣 - It has already been paid into the treasury yesterday.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 门第 - Refers to social status or family background, particularly in the context of marriage.
  • 媵妾 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife, indicating a lower social status compared to a primary wife.
  • 姓字年庚 - Refers to one's name, age, and other personal details, often used in formal contexts.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, often used as a form of currency or for valuable items.
  • 修葺费 - Refers to the cost of repairs or renovations.
  • 仍旧贯 - Means to maintain the old ways or traditions.
  • 封粮之由单 - Refers to a receipt for grain tax, indicating a financial transaction.
  • 投柜完纳 - Refers to paying into the treasury, a common practice in historical contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 卿 - A respectful term for a person, often used in formal or literary contexts.
  • 贫生 - Refers to a poor scholar, indicating a lower social status.
  • 媵妾 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, often used as a form of currency or for valuable items.
  • 修葺费 - Refers to the cost of repairs or renovations.
  • 仍旧贯 - Means to maintain the old ways or traditions.
  • 封粮之由单 - Refers to a receipt for grain tax.
  • 投柜完纳 - Refers to paying into the treasury.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何至家 - Indicates that someone named He returned home.
  • 妻正异其旬日不归 - An idiomatic expression meaning 'his wife was surprised by his absence for ten days.'
  • 有从者移运财物 - Means 'there were attendants moving his possessions.'
  • 不便致询 - Means 'he did not feel it necessary to inquire.'
  • 何始向周述相遇之奇 - Means 'He then told Zhou about the strange encounter.'
  • 周闻之，深德女 - Means 'Zhou heard this and deeply appreciated the woman.'
  • 急欲一见，议欲往接 - Means 'he was eager to meet her and considered going to greet her.'
  • 勿庸，半月将自至 - Means 'It's not necessary; she will come in half a month.'
  • 君能待乎 - Means 'Can you wait?'
  • 能不能卿亦素知，无问我 - Means 'Whether you can or not, you already know, so don't ask me.'
  • 何急缮房屋，旬日告竣 - Means 'He quickly prepared the house, and it was completed in ten days.'
  • 女果至 - Means 'The woman indeed arrived.'
  • 女素服淡妆，无事华饰 - Means 'She wore plain clothes and light makeup, without any extravagant decorations.'
  • 见周，欲行嫡庶礼 - Means 'When she saw Zhou, she wanted to perform the formalities of a wife.'
  • 周止之，遂以平礼见 - Means 'Zhou stopped her, and they greeted each other with simple courtesy.'
  • 芳容若是，勿怪郎君言念不置 - Means 'Her beauty is so captivating, it's no wonder the young man spoke of her constantly.'
  • 女携来衣服衾帐等物，皆有两副，悉与周分用之 - Means 'The woman brought clothes, bedding, and other items, all in pairs, and they were used by both of them.'
  • 后女连生二子，周不育，以女之子为己子，珍爱犹女 - Means 'Later, the woman gave birth to two sons. Zhou could not have children, so he treated the woman's sons as his own, cherishing them as if they were his daughters.'
  • 女每归省，往来必以半月，即与何同往，亦不愆期 - Means 'The woman would visit home every month, and her visits lasted half a month. Even when she went with He, she never missed her schedule.'
  • 然每回家，携带钱物不下千馀金，何因而巨富 - Means 'However, every time she returned home, she would bring back thousands of gold pieces, which made He become extremely wealthy.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 旬日 - Refers to ten days, a common unit of time in classical Chinese.
  • 从者 - Refers to attendants or servants.
  • 嫡庶礼 - Refers to the formalities between a husband and wife, particularly in the context of a marriage.
  • 平礼 - Refers to simple courtesy, as opposed to formal or elaborate rituals.
  • 芳容 - Refers to beautiful appearance, often used to describe women.
  • 郎君 - A term of endearment for a young man, often used by older women.
  • 归省 - Refers to visiting one's family, often used in the context of a married woman returning to her parents' home.

Proper Nouns

  • 何 - A proper noun referring to a person named He.
  • 周 - A proper noun referring to a person named Zhou.
  • 女 - A proper noun referring to a woman.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日 - Time marker meaning 'one day' or 'on one day.'
  • 弄子為樂 - Playful interaction with a child.
  • 胡差人迎女 - Strangers sent to fetch the woman.
  • 酒筵已具 - The banquet was already prepared.
  • 就坐后 - After taking their seats.
  • 積镪市产 - Accumulated wealth and purchased property.
  • 文契 - Legal papers for property.
  • 实告君 - I will tell you the truth.
  • 狐也 - A fox, referring to the speaker's identity.
  • 修道 - To cultivate the Dao, referring to spiritual or religious practice.
  • 潸然泣下 - Tears welling up, describing emotional distress.
  • 行期尚未定 - The departure date is not yet set.
  • 半年后或未晚也 - Perhaps it's not too late in half a year.
  • 终不乐 - Ultimately unhappy.
  • 席终各寝 - After the banquet, they went to bed.
  • 早起省亲 - Got up early to visit relatives.
  • 不知翁媪去向 - Did not know where the elders had gone.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡 - Refers to strangers or outsiders, often used in a neutral or slightly negative sense in classical Chinese.
  • 酒筵 - Refers to a formal banquet or feast, a common social event in classical Chinese culture.
  • 修道 - Refers to the practice of cultivating the Dao, a central concept in Chinese philosophy and religion.
  • 潸然泣下 - Describes a state of emotional distress, often associated with deep feelings or sorrow.
  • 半年后 - Refers to a period of six months, a common timeframe in classical Chinese for planning or waiting.

Proper Nouns

  • 女 - The woman, a key character in the story.
  • 何 - The man, the woman's husband or partner.
  • 胡 - The strangers, a group of people who arrive unexpectedly.
  • 翁媪 - The elders, likely the woman's parents or other older relatives.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遭遇如是 - Phrase meaning 'to encounter such circumstances' or 'to face such situations,' often used to introduce a situation or problem.
  • 人之所同欲也 - Means 'what all people desire,' indicating a universal human aspiration.
  • 假非纯诚 - Means 'if not with pure sincerity,' implying that sincerity is a necessary condition.
  • 何有坐享之福 - Means 'how can there be a fortune to enjoy without effort,' suggesting that effortless gains are not possible.
  • 狐虽能福人 - A metaphorical expression where '狐' (fox) symbolizes someone who can bring good fortune.
  • 亦由人之自致 - Means 'it is also due to one's own efforts,' emphasizing personal responsibility.
  • 岂有私心于其间哉 - Means 'how could there be any selfish motives involved?' questioning the presence of ulterior motives.
  • 事似涉幻 - Means 'the matter seems to involve illusion or fantasy,' indicating a sense of unreality.
  • 文极显明 - Means 'the text is extremely clear and explicit,' praising the clarity of the writing.
  • 斯为妙笔 - Means 'this is a masterful stroke,' praising the quality of the writing.
  • 有德者必有福 - Means 'those with virtue will surely have good fortune,' a common moral statement.
  • 事奇理正 - Means 'the matter is strange but the principle is correct,' acknowledging the unusual nature of the event while affirming its moral correctness.
  • 是谓奇而法 - Means 'this is called strange yet lawful,' suggesting that the event is both unusual and morally justified.
  • 上元李瑜谨注 - A signature indicating the author or commentator, '李瑜' (Li Yu) from the '上元' (Shangyuan) period.
  • 鬼联 - Refers to a ghostly couplet or a ghostly connection.
  • 某郡署 - Refers to a government office in a certain county.
  • 恒闻吟哦声曰 - Means 'constantly hear the recitation sound saying,' describing a recurring auditory phenomenon.
  • 相沿久，弗之怪也 - Means 'it has been passed down for a long time, and no one finds it strange,' indicating a long-standing acceptance of the phenomenon.
  • 太守钱公新莅任 - Means 'Governor Qian has newly taken office,' introducing a new official.
  • 及曙 - Means 'until dawn,' indicating the time of day.
  • 集署人问焉 - Means 'gathered the officials in the office to ask about it,' describing a formal inquiry.
  • 盖十年前 - Means 'about ten years ago,' providing a time reference.
  • 某守夫人深通文墨 - Means 'the wife of a certain governor was deeply versed in literature and writing,' describing her literary skills.
  • 夜半偶得此联 - Means 'at midnight, she happened to compose this couplet,' describing the origin of the couplet.
  • 属对不能 - Means 'unable to find a matching line,' indicating a challenge in completing the couplet.
  • 以此致疾而殁 - Means 'because of this, she fell ill and died,' describing the consequence of her inability to complete the couplet.
  • 公嗟叹良久 - Means 'the governor sighed for a long time,' expressing deep reflection.
  • 会中秋夜饮 - Means 'coincidentally, during a Mid-Autumn night gathering,' setting the scene.
  • 客既散 - Means 'after the guests had left,' indicating the end of the gathering.
  • 心恋月华 - Means 'still fond of the moonlight,' describing the governor's lingering appreciation.
  • 低徊阶畔 - Means 'lingering by the steps,' indicating a reflective moment.
  • 闻鬼言如前 - Means 'heard the ghostly voice as before,' describing the recurrence of the auditory phenomenon.
  • 今夕得对句矣 - Means 'today, I have found the matching line,' indicating the resolution of the challenge.
  • ‘中秋八月中’ - The completed couplet, 'Mid-Autumn in the middle of August,' providing the solution.
  • 可璧合否 - Means 'can it be perfectly matched?' asking for confirmation.
  • 自此鬼言遂止 - Means 'from then on, the ghostly voice ceased,' indicating the resolution of the phenomenon.
  • 以一联致疾而殁，斯亦愚矣 - Means 'to die because of a single couplet, this is foolish,' criticizing the excessive attachment to the couplet.
  • 予智自雄 - Means 'to think oneself superior,' describing arrogance.
  • 而于不能者甘于不能 - Means 'and to be content with one's inability,' indicating a lack of effort.
  • 反谓所学高美 - Means 'instead, to claim that one's learning is high and beautiful,' describing self-deception.
  • 而为人所不能及 - Means 'and that others cannot match,' indicating a false sense of superiority.
  • 以故终身无进境 - Means 'therefore, one's life is without progress,' describing the negative consequences of such attitudes.
  • 则不愚者之有愧于愚者多矣 - Means 'thus, those who are not foolish have much to be ashamed of,' suggesting a moral judgment.
  • 若夫人者，谓之好学深思也亦可 - Means 'as for her, she can be called a good learner and deep thinker,' offering a positive evaluation despite the negative outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虚白道人 - A pseudonym or title for a Daoist or Buddhist monk, often used to add a spiritual or philosophical dimension to the text.
  • 侯仲霖 - A name that might refer to a historical figure or a contemporary person, adding a personal touch to the commentary.
  • 上元李瑜谨注 - A signature indicating the author or commentator, '李瑜' (Li Yu) from the '上元' (Shangyuan) period, a specific era in Chinese history.
  • 鬼联 - Refers to a ghostly couplet, a literary device often used in Chinese literature to add a supernatural or mystical element.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文章本天成，妙手偶得之 - A poetic phrase meaning 'literature is naturally formed, and a skilled hand occasionally captures it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冤缢 - A common theme in classical Chinese literature, often used to highlight moral or social issues.
  • 才鬼传 - Refers to the *Talented Ghosts*, a collection of stories that often feature talented but deceased individuals, reflecting the cultural belief in the afterlife and the value of talent.

Proper Nouns

  • 甲某 - A character named Jia.
  • 乙某 - A character named Yi.
  • 上元李瑜 - The author, Li Yu, from the Shangyuan period.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后甲、乙同年入泮 - Refers to two individuals, '甲' and '乙,' who entered the school (泮) in the same year.
  • 俱入乡试 - Means 'both entered the provincial examination,' indicating that they both took the next level of the imperial examination system.
  • 贡院中有桐树一株 - Means 'the examination hall has a paulownia tree,' where '贡院' refers to the examination hall and '桐树' is a specific tree, likely a symbol of integrity and virtue in Chinese culture.
  • 中秋夜监临椅坐树下 - Means 'on the Mid-Autumn Festival night, the examiner sat on a chair under the tree,' where '监临' refers to the examiner or supervisor overseeing the examination and '中秋夜' is the Mid-Autumn Festival night.
  • 移椅倚桐同玩月 - A poetic phrase meaning 'move the chair, lean against the paulownia, and enjoy the moon together,' describing a beautiful and evocative scene.
  • 等灯登阁各观书 - A poetic phrase meaning 'wait for the lamp, climb the tower, and each read a book,' a clever and balanced response to the earlier phrase.
  • 坐睡树下 - Means 'sitting and sleeping under the tree,' indicating a state of relaxation or drowsiness.
  • 梦一美人曰 - Means 'dreamed of a beautiful woman who said,' introducing a dream sequence.
  • 妾有一联 - Means 'I have a couplet,' indicating that the woman in the dream has a response to the examiner's phrase.
  • 不知可对否 - Means 'do you think it can be a match?' indicating a question of whether the response is appropriate.
  • 监临见美人项系绳而舌出 - Means 'the examiner saw the beautiful woman with a rope around her neck and her tongue hanging out,' indicating that the woman was a ghost.
  • 因惊寤 - Means 'he was startled awake,' indicating that the examiner woke up from the dream.
  • 知为含冤缢鬼 - Means 'he knew it was a ghost of someone who had been wrongfully strangled,' indicating that the woman in the dream was a ghost seeking justice.
  • 立差官入场取对 - Means 'he immediately sent an official into the examination hall to collect the answers,' indicating a swift action taken by the examiner.
  • 内有甲、乙二人所对雷同 - Means 'among them, the answers of 甲 and 乙 were identical,' indicating that the two individuals had the same response.
  • 实即缢鬼联也 - Means 'it was actually the couplet of the strangled ghost,' indicating that the response was the same as the one given by the ghost in the dream.
  • 意鬼之缢，必由此对而致 - Means 'he thought that the ghost's death must have been caused by this couplet,' indicating a connection between the couplet and the ghost's death.
  • 鬼之冤，亦必由此对而伸 - Means 'the ghost's injustice must also be resolved by this couplet,' indicating that the couplet would bring justice to the ghost.
  • 因暗记甲、乙府邑姓名 - Means 'he secretly recorded the names and places of origin of 甲 and 乙,' indicating that the examiner kept track of the two individuals.
  • 榜后，将甲、乙传至，委官问之 - Means 'after the results were announced, he summoned 甲 and 乙 and had an official question them,' indicating the final step in the process.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '云曰：“吝耳！何恐之有？妾已带酒来矣。”' is a direct speech with a playful tone, where the speaker (云) is teasing the listener (孙).
  • The exchange '孙曰：“安在？”曰：“此其非耶？”' is ambiguous. '安在' means 'where is it?' and '此其非耶' means 'is this not it?' The exact meaning of the second part is unclear.
  • The sentence '见酒具自外飞入，若有人捧托，不见其人。' describes a magical or supernatural event where wine utensils fly in, seemingly carried by an unseen person.
  • The phrase '杯箸肴果，一一如是。' emphasizes that all the utensils and food items enter in the same manner.
  • The sentence '孙奇之，曰：“反宾作主矣！”' shows the listener's surprise and the reversal of roles.
  • The sentence '云微笑。孙此际饮同佳丽，倚偎谈笑，小登科之乐不及此。' describes the scene where the two are drinking, talking, and laughing, with the listener finding the current joy greater than a minor success.
  • The sentence '昨睹卿面，盼望殷切，不料卿应念而至，小生何修而得此？' is a direct speech where the listener expresses surprise and gratitude for the speaker's unexpected arrival.
  • The sentence '妾与君有宿分，即君弗盼望妾亦自至，以了其缘，盖恐迟则无及耳。' explains the predestined fate and the urgency of meeting.
  • The sentence '此事君务慎密，不可以告人，倘风声播扬，妾亦不便来矣。切嘱，切嘱！' is a warning to keep the relationship secret.
  • The sentence '且君体固弱，妾亦不宜屡至，当来则来，勿悬望也。' explains the reasons for the infrequent visits due to the listener's weak body.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 虚白道人曰 - Classical phrase meaning 'the Daoist Master Xubai said,' introducing a statement attributed to a Daoist figure.
  • 美哉仙乎 - Exclamatory phrase meaning 'How beautiful the fairy is!' used to express admiration.
  • 云为高伦类矣 - Phrase meaning 'Cloud is superior to all kinds of beings,' emphasizing Cloud's exceptional status.
  • 非节制嗜欲、敦笃恩义者不能也 - Phrase meaning 'Only those who can restrain their desires and cultivate deep affection can do this,' highlighting the qualities required for such behavior.
  • 送肴酒暗为应客，逢落帽明为续带 - Phrase describing Cloud's actions: 'secretly serving food and wine to entertain guests, and openly fixing a fallen hat,' illustrating her skillful and considerate behavior.
  • 非无违夫意、善执妇功者不能 - Phrase meaning 'Only those who do not defy their husband's wishes and are good at household duties can do this,' emphasizing the qualities required for such behavior.
  • 为代唱，度兰君之幽魂 - Phrase meaning 'to sing on behalf of Lanjun's soul,' indicating a specific act of remembrance or tribute.
  • 了宿缘，知孙郎之寿数 - Phrase meaning 'to understand the karmic connection and know Sun Lang's lifespan,' suggesting a deeper understanding of fate and destiny.
  • 窥其微，即无起死回生之术 - Phrase meaning 'to see the subtle signs, but without the power to bring the dead back to life,' indicating the limits of Cloud's abilities.
  • 若责以坐视夫病而不救，不无小补，惜友人思虑之未及此也 - Phrase meaning 'If one were to blame her for not helping her husband when he was ill, it would be a minor benefit, but it is a pity that my friend did not consider this,' expressing a nuanced view of Cloud's actions.

  • 闻诸生会饮，必敬备肴酒使人送去 - Means 'when he hears that scholars are having a drinking party, he always respectfully prepares food and wine and sends someone to deliver it,' indicating a respectful and generous gesture.

  • 紅妝坐帳 - 'Dressed in red and sitting in the bridal canopy,' a vivid description of a bride's appearance and setting.
  • 群婢侍立左右 - 'A group of maids standing on either side,' indicating the presence of attendants in the bridal chamber.
  • 不覺失言 - 'Unconsciously spoke out,' suggesting a moment of spontaneous or unintended expression.
  • 吾何修而得此 - 'What virtue have I practiced to attain this?' A rhetorical question expressing humility or surprise.
  • 大丈夫之遭遇 - 'The fate of a true man,' referring to the fortune or destiny of a man of high moral standing.
  • 朝為田舍郎，暮登天子堂 - A well-known proverb meaning 'From a farmer in the morning to a minister of the emperor in the evening,' symbolizing a sudden rise in social status.
  • 即食前方丈，侍妾數百人 - 'Even eating with a full banquet before you and having hundreds of concubines to serve you,' an exaggerated description of wealth and power.
  • 分内事耳 - 'Matters within one's reach,' implying that such success is not extraordinary.
  • 叨列婚姻 - 'To be honored by marriage,' expressing gratitude for the union.
  • 未知世系 - 'Unaware of the family lineage,' indicating a lack of knowledge about the other's background.
  • 此屬憾事 - 'This is a regrettable matter,' expressing dissatisfaction or disappointment.
  • 恐君驚訝 - 'Afraid that you might be startled,' showing concern for the other's reaction.
  • 即舉家鬼狐 - 'Even if the entire family were ghosts or fox spirits,' a hyperbolic statement to reassure the listener.
  • 獨非 - 'Not being one of them,' indicating a distinction from the rest.
  • 曾侍郎 - 'Former Censor,' a title indicating a high-ranking official in the past.
  • 實妾生父也 - 'He is my biological father,' a direct statement of parentage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洞房 - The bridal chamber, a traditional setting for the consummation of marriage in Chinese culture.
  • 紅妝 - Red makeup, a symbol of a bride's beauty and readiness for marriage.
  • 群婢 - Maids or servants, indicating the social status and wealth of the family.
  • 田舍郎 - A farmer or peasant, representing the lowest social class.
  • 天子堂 - The hall of the emperor, symbolizing the highest social and political status.
  • 食前方丈 - A banquet with food spread out before you, indicating great wealth and luxury.
  • 侍妾數百人 - Hundreds of concubines, a sign of immense wealth and power.
  • 曾侍郎 - A high-ranking official, indicating a prestigious family background.
  • 狐 - Foxes, often associated with supernatural beings in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 應 - Ying, the male protagonist.
  • 新人 - The bride.
  • 曾侍郎 - A high-ranking official, the bride's biological father.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 應在陳家設帳五年 - Metaphor for teaching or working as a tutor at Chen's house for five years.
  • 妻已一胎生雙子矣 - Literal description of a rare occurrence where the wife has given birth to twins in one pregnancy.
  • 陳薄具酒酌 - Chen prepared wine for a toast; '薄具' means 'prepared' or 'arranged,' and '酒酌' refers to drinking wine.
  • 與應夫婦同酌共話 - Toasted together with Ying and his wife; '同酌共話' means 'toasting and chatting together.'
  • 仆祖居湖北 - '仆' is a humble term for 'I' or 'myself'; the speaker's ancestors lived in Hubei.
  • 家中尚有二子一孙 - Indicates that the speaker has two sons and a grandson at home.
  • 为女故，居此五载 - Explains the reason for the speaker's prolonged stay because of the daughter.
  • 今将旋归 - '旋归' means 'return home'; the speaker will return home now.
  • 且贤婿恶运已过，美运继至 - Way of saying that the son-in-law's bad luck has passed, and good fortune is to follow.
  • 车已雇妥，明晨可以早发 - '雇妥' means 'hired and arranged'; the carriage has been hired, and they can leave early tomorrow morning.
  • 具白金一千五百两为赆 - '白金' refers to silver, and '赆' is a gift given to someone who is leaving; 1,500 taels of silver as a gift.
  • 五百赠女 - Specific amount given to the daughter; 500 taels of silver.
  • 五年书金支使有限 - Indicates that the financial support provided was not unlimited; the 5 years' salary and expenses were limited.
  • 另具银若干在此 - Suggests that there is more money available beyond the initial gift; additionally, some silver is here.
  • 携带而归，可无恨鸡鸣之早矣 - Way of saying that the money will be useful and appreciated; take it back, and you won't regret waking up early.
  • 应夫妇闻之，不胜酸楚 - '不胜' means 'unable to bear' or 'extremely'; Ying and his wife were very sad to hear it.
  • 勿为此儿女之态也 - Way of telling them to be mature and composed; don't act like children.
  • 时已薄暮，汝夫妇可急整行装 - '薄暮' means 'late afternoon' or 'dusk'; it is already late afternoon, you two should quickly pack your belongings.
  • 甫毕，车已到门 - '甫毕' means 'just finished'; just finished, the carriage has arrived at the door.
  • 临行，陈以五色布袱授女 - On the way out, Chen gave the daughter a five-colored cloth bundle; '五色布袱' is a colorful cloth bundle.
  • 长短如被 - Describes the size of the cloth bundle; as long and wide as a quilt.
  • 履之，数千里之遥可顷刻而至 - Magical or metaphorical statement; wear it, and you can travel thousands of miles in an instant.
  • 授以咒语 - Taught her a spell; '咒语' is a spell or incantation, often associated with magic or supernatural powers.
  • 勿轻用，勿传人 - Warning to be careful with the magical item; do not use it lightly, do not tell others.
  • 立视升车，依依而别 - Went up the carriage, and they parted with reluctance; '依依' means 'reluctantly' or 'with affection.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 应 - Name of the tutor or teacher.
  • 陈 - Name of the host or patron who is sending the tutor and his family back home.
  • 湖北 - Province in China, indicating the speaker's origin.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, a valuable metal used as currency in ancient times.
  • 五色布袱 - Colorful cloth bundle, possibly a magical or symbolic item.
  • 咒语 - Spell or incantation, often associated with magic or supernatural powers.
  • 依依而别 - Common phrase in Chinese literature describing a reluctant parting.

Proper Nouns

  • 应 - Name of the tutor or teacher.
  • 陈 - Name of the host or patron.
  • 湖北 - Province in China.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver.
  • 五色布袱 - Colorful cloth bundle.
  • 咒语 - Spell or incantation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 應舉于鄉 - Refers to taking the provincial examination, a key step in the imperial examination system.
  • 丁憂家居 - A traditional practice of mourning and staying at home after the death of a parent.
  • 认亲 - Means 'to recognize kinship' or 'to acknowledge familial ties.'
  • 何以知曾公为卿父 - A rhetorical question meaning 'How can you know that Mr. Zeng is your father?'
  • 抱来难割爱，捐去倍钟情 - Expresses the emotional conflict of adoption and abandonment.
  • 梦枉蛇祥叶，心期鸟覆成 - Likely refers to a dream or omen involving snakes and birds, symbolizing fate or destiny.
  • 他年如聚晤，持此证分明 - Means 'In the future, if we meet again, this will serve as clear proof.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The imperial examination system, a key institution in Chinese governance and social mobility.
  • 丁忧, a traditional practice of mourning and staying at home after the death of a parent, rooted in Confucian filial piety.
  • 认亲, the practice of acknowledging kinship, often involving complex social and familial relationships.
  • Dreams are often interpreted as omens or signs of fate in Chinese culture.
  • 蛇 and 鸟 are often used in Chinese folklore and literature as symbols of transformation or fate.

Proper Nouns

  • 应 - The name of the protagonist, a man who is taking the provincial examination.
  • 陈氏 - The name of 应's wife, who is seeking to recognize her father.
  • 曾公 - The name of the man whose identity is being questioned, likely a relative or acquaintance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 似为生女而弃之也者 - Phrase meaning 'it seems to be the case of a girl being abandoned,' a complex construction in Classical Chinese.
  • 究未知其原因 - Phrase meaning 'the exact reason is still unknown,' indicating a lack of clarity.
  • 遂使侍婢以诗呈公 - Phrase meaning 'so he had a servant girl present the poem to him,' where '遂' indicates a result or consequence.
  • 既而，公持诗来言曰 - Phrase meaning 'afterwards, the official came with the poem and said,' where '既而' indicates a sequence of events.
  • 尚记汝之生辰乎 - Phrase meaning 'do you still remember your birthdate?' where '尚记' means 'still remember.'

  • 遂谓夫人曰 - Phrase meaning 'then he told his wife,' where '遂' again indicates a result.
  • 此弃诸路侧之女也 - Phrase meaning 'this is the girl who was abandoned on the roadside,' indicating a specific detail.
  • 吾女肘后有红记如钱 - Phrase meaning 'my daughter has a red mark on her elbow like a coin,' where '红记如钱' describes a distinctive mark.
  • 盖道姑返俗归曾 - Phrase meaning 'it turns out that the nun returned to the secular world and married into the Zeng family,' where '盖' introduces a conclusion.
  • 即陈氏之生母也 - Phrase meaning 'she is the birth mother of Chen,' where '即' means 'is' or 'is precisely.'
  • 曾夫妇大喜 - Phrase meaning 'the Zeng couple was very happy,' where '大喜' means 'very happy.'
  • 如爱女之再生 - Phrase meaning 'as if their daughter had been reborn,' indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 改陈氏为曾氏 - Phrase meaning 'they changed Chen's surname to Zeng,' where '改' means 'changed' and '为' means 'to.'
  • 遂问抱养之详 - Phrase meaning 'then they asked about the details of the adoption,' where '遂' indicates a result.
  • 曾氏止讳言陈公为狐 - Phrase meaning 'the Zeng family only hesitated to mention that Chen Gong was a fox,' where '止讳言' means 'only hesitated to mention.'
  • 其馀一一细述 - Phrase meaning 'and they described the rest in detail,' where '一一细述' means 'described in detail.'
  • 曾喜之不胜 - Phrase meaning 'the Zeng couple was overjoyed,' where '不胜' means 'overjoyed' or 'exceedingly.'
  • 明日汝夫妇同来 - Phrase meaning 'come tomorrow with your husband,' where '汝夫妇' means 'you and your husband.'
  • 如三晨谢亲之礼 - Phrase meaning 'as if it were the third morning of a thanksgiving ritual,' where '三晨' refers to a specific ritual.
  • 万勿草草 - Phrase meaning 'do not be careless,' where '万勿' means 'do not' and '草草' means 'careless.'

  • 行翁婿礼 - Phrase meaning 'they performed the ritual of a father-in-law and son-in-law,' where '行' means 'perform' and '翁婿礼' means 'the ritual of a father-in-law and son-in-law.'
  • 留之信宿 - Phrase meaning 'they stayed for two nights,' where '信宿' means 'two nights.'

  • 陪送之物载以后车数乘 - Phrase meaning 'the gifts were loaded onto several subsequent carriages,' where '陪送之物' means 'gifts' and '后车数乘' means 'several subsequent carriages.'

  • 侍婢 - Refers to a servant girl, a common figure in classical Chinese literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 书中自有颜如玉 - Classical expression meaning 'within the book there is a beauty like jade,' often used to encourage study and learning.
  • 吾闻其语矣，今见其人也 - Means 'I have heard of this saying, and now I see the person,' indicating a transition from hearsay to personal experience.
  • 变幻离奇处见造化，惨澹经营处见文心 - 'In the strange and marvelous transformations, one sees the work of nature; in the painstaking efforts, one sees the writer's heart.' Highlights the interplay between natural phenomena and human creativity.
  • 酒色之杀人，甚于作直 - Means 'the killing power of wine and women is greater than that of being straightforward,' a commentary on the dangers of indulgence versus integrity.
  • 为酒色死，人不为悔，逆畏以直致祸 - 'To die for wine and women, people do not regret it, but they fear that being straightforward will bring disaster.' Reflects a societal attitude towards indulgence and integrity.
  • 此由心不正直，故以苟且为明哲耳 - 'This is because the heart is not upright, so they consider compromise as wisdom.' A critique of moral compromise.
  • 读是篇而知正直之人固为神之所福者也 - 'Reading this passage, one knows that upright people are indeed blessed by the gods.' Emphasizes the divine favor for integrity.
  • 然正直如先生，而未为神之所福也，何居 - 'Yet, although upright like the master, he was not blessed by the gods. Why is that?' A rhetorical question challenging the notion of divine justice.
  • 上元李瑜谨注 - 'Shangyuan Li Yu respectfully annotates,' indicating the author of the annotation.
  • 乾隆壬辰 - The year 1782 during the reign of the Qianlong Emperor.
  • 某抚宪奉旨登岱祭碧霞元君 - 'A certain governor-general, upon imperial command, ascended Mount Tai to worship the Lady of the Azure Clouds.'
  • 县尹某立即出示 - 'The county magistrate immediately issued an announcement.'
  • 遐迩进香之人，不许是日上山 - 'People from far and near who come to offer incense are not allowed to ascend the mountain on this day.'
  • 且使人扫除殿宇，务令清洁 - 'Moreover, he had the temple buildings cleaned, ensuring they were spotless.'
  • 县尹先行，复于大殿大肆陈设毕 - 'The county magistrate went first, then set up elaborate decorations in the main hall.'
  • 始请抚宪拈香 - 'Finally, he invited the governor-general to light incense.'

  • 县尹大骇，旋见神案上有单帖一纸，上书商河某里居幼女宋蕙娘遥祭 - 'The county magistrate was greatly startled, and then saw a single sheet of paper on the altar, which read that a young girl named Song Hui娘 from a certain village in Shanghe had made a remote offering.'
  • 抚宪执向县尹曰：“既曰遥祭，则焚纸锞者并未到此，其中必有神佑。” - 'The governor-general took the paper and said to the county magistrate, 'Since it is a remote offering, the person who burned the paper offerings did not come here, and there must be divine protection.' Suggesting supernatural intervention.
  • 转瞬帖、灰俱杳，抚宪不胜惊异 - 'In a moment, the paper and ashes disappeared, and the governor-general was greatly astonished.'
  • 祭事毕，回省，札谕商河县查访其事 - 'After the worship ceremony, he returned to the province and issued an order to investigate the matter in Shanghe County.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is a complex rhetorical question that links the candidate's personal efforts in the six arts to his failure in the examination, suggesting that it might be fate.
  • This sentence contrasts the overall quality of the students with the specific case of the candidate, implying that the failure was not due to the writing itself.
  • This is a conditional statement that offers to help the teacher achieve a favorable outcome by setting up a school.
  • This sentence explains the teacher's reluctance to repeat past mistakes but also shows a willingness to take the opportunity for a personal reason.
  • This is a rhetorical question that emphasizes the teacher's vow not to read or study, even more so when it comes to taking the examination.

  • 黄秀之录君文而中也 - 'When Huang Xiu records your writing and it succeeds,' suggesting that success can be attributed to external factors.
  • 未试之他人也 - 'Not tested by others,' indicating that the success might be due to chance or the particularity of the person.
  • 君自作自录而不中也 - 'When you yourself write and record it and it fails,' suggesting that failure is due to the quality of the work.
  • 文中尚有瑕疵 - 'There may still be flaws in the writing,' indicating that the failure could be due to imperfections in the work.
  • 男儿当自强 - Well-known saying meaning 'A man should strive to be strong,' encouraging perseverance and self-improvement.
  • 不能扬名声以显父母 - 'Unable to promote one's reputation to honor one's parents,' reflecting the Confucian value of filial piety.
  • 是无父子也 - 'This is no father and son,' suggesting a breakdown in the filial relationship.
  • 不能衣紫服朱 - 'Unable to wear purple and red robes,' referring to the status and honor of holding an official position.
  • 是无君臣也 - 'This is no ruler and subject,' suggesting a breakdown in the hierarchical relationship.
  • 谠言正论而不听 - 'Not listening to sincere and correct advice,' indicating a failure to heed good counsel.
  • 是无朋友也 - 'This is no friend,' suggesting a breakdown in the friendship.
  • 不能得一官半职以封赠妻室 - 'Unable to obtain a minor official position to honor one's wife,' reflecting the importance of achieving social status for the family.
  • 是无夫妇也 - 'This is no marital relationship,' suggesting a breakdown in the marital relationship.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 君命薄 - Reflects the traditional Chinese belief in fate and destiny, where one's success or failure is often attributed to external forces.
  • 男儿当自强 - Well-known saying from the *Book of Rites* (礼记), emphasizing the importance of personal effort and perseverance.
  • 扬名声以显父母 - Reflects the Confucian value of filial piety, where a son's success is seen as a way to honor his parents.
  • 衣紫服朱 - Purple and red were colors associated with high-ranking officials in ancient China, symbolizing status and honor.
  • 谠言正论 - Refers to sincere and correct advice, a concept important in Confucian ethics.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄秀 - A name, possibly referring to a specific person who recorded the husband's writing.
  • 君 - Refers to the husband.
  • 妾 - Refers to the wife.
  • 父母 - Refers to the husband's parents.
  • 君臣 - Refers to the hierarchical relationship between ruler and subject.
  • 朋友 - Refers to the relationship between friends.
  • 夫妇 - Refers to the marital relationship.

  • 悬梁自缢 - This is a reference to a traditional method of suicide in Chinese culture, often associated with extreme emotional distress.
  • 反悲为喜 - This phrase reflects the emotional transformation from sorrow to happiness, a common theme in classical literature.

  • 如顾某之功苦，贾用不售 - Comparison meaning 'Just as Mr. Gu's efforts were arduous, yet his talent went unappreciated.'
  • 其不免于悲叹也宜矣 - Statement of inevitability meaning 'It is indeed appropriate that he could not escape sorrow and lamentation.'
  • 文章无止境，当益求其奥妙 - Statement about the nature of writing meaning 'Literature has no limits; one should strive to explore its profound mysteries.'
  • 功名有定时，不可必之目前 - Statement about the timing of success meaning 'Success in career and reputation has its time; it cannot be guaranteed in the present moment.'
  • 乃以人录其文而捷，遂以己之不中归于命数 - Statement of cause and effect meaning 'He attributed his success to others recording his writings and thus attributed his own failure to fate.'
  • 直谓命中无是功名，致欲尽弃前功，甘老林泉 - Statement of resignation meaning 'He simply believed that he was not destined for such success, leading him to abandon all previous efforts and content himself with a life in the mountains and forests.'
  • 岂不可惜！ - Expression of regret meaning 'How regrettable!'
  • 幸有贤妻以死劝之，遂致联捷 - Statement of intervention meaning 'Fortunately, his virtuous wife, by sacrificing herself, persuaded him, and he subsequently achieved success.'
  • 不然亦止为一时狂士耳 - Hypothetical conclusion meaning 'Otherwise, he would have remained just a momentary madman.'

  • 顾某 - A reference to an unnamed individual, likely a historical or literary figure. '顾某' means 'Mr. Gu' or 'someone named Gu.'
  • 功苦 - A term describing arduous efforts, often used in classical texts to describe the hard work and dedication of scholars or artists.
  • 贾用不售 - A term describing unappreciated talent, often used to describe the plight of talented individuals who are not recognized for their abilities.

  • 联捷 - A term referring to subsequent success, often used to describe a series of achievements or promotions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日 - A common temporal marker meaning 'one day' or 'on a certain day.'
  • 其价甚廉 - 'Its price was very cheap,' a straightforward expression.
  • 清不欲 - 'Qing did not want,' a simple negation.
  • 复加二千，清仍不欲 - 'Again added two thousand, but Qing still did not want.' This repetition emphasizes Qing's reluctance.
  • 农人乃止 - 'The farmer then stopped,' indicating the end of the negotiation.
  • 清货于本集回民 - 'Qing sold the cow to a Hui person in this gathering place.' This specifies the transaction.
  • 较农人加价多得数百文 - 'Earned hundreds of coins more than the farmer.' This compares the prices.
  • 回以是集牛脔甚少而价昂 - 'The Hui person thought the pieces of beef in this gathering place were few and the price high.' This explains the Hui person's action.
  • 遂立杀是牛而货之 - 'So immediately killed the cow and sold it.' This describes the Hui person's decision.
  • 清持价归，其意得甚 - 'Qing carried the money home, feeling very pleased.' This shows Qing's emotional state.
  • 甫至家，即有二役执票来拘 - 'Just as he arrived home, two officials came with a warrant to arrest him.' This introduces a sudden change of events.
  • 谁人控我？ - 'Who accused me?' Qing's question.
  • 汝自作之事，尚不知耶？ - 'You don't even know what you did?' The official's sarcastic response.
  • 乃缧绁其项，牵之去 - 'They then bound his neck and dragged him away.' This describes the arrest.
  • 二役在路苛索差礼 - 'The two officials demanded extra fees on the way.' This shows their behavior.
  • 吾身边分文未有，奈何？ - 'I don't have a single coin, what can I do?' Qing's plea.
  • 当衙役者吸风度日耶？ - 'Do officials live by breathing air?' The officials' angry response.
  • 无已，俟结案回家时加倍奉酬 - 'If not, I will double the payment when I return home after the case is settled.' Qing's promise.
  • 遂格外多许之，役乃喜 - 'So he promised extra, and the officials were pleased.' This shows the resolution.
  • 路经一山，见山下有牛若干，口吐人言，向清索命 - 'On the way, they passed a mountain and saw several cows at the foot of the mountain, speaking human words and demanding Qing's life.' This introduces a supernatural element.
  • 即为是案传来，于汝等明冤，未经过堂，汝等不得无礼 - 'Since you were summoned for this case, you must be innocent. Before the trial, you cannot be disrespectful.' The officials' reasoning.
  • 众牛乃散 - 'The cows then dispersed.' This ends the supernatural encounter.
  • 清始知已死 - 'Qing then realized he was dead.' This concludes the story.

  • 纵己物而宰于私，亦官刑而使之戒 - Means 'even if the ox belongs to oneself and is slaughtered privately, it is still subject to official punishment and serves as a warning,' where '纵己物' means 'even if it belongs to oneself.'

  • 夫卖剑而买之者，为犁雨耕云之助；而鼓刀而割之者，启逾墙穿穴之萌 - Means 'those who sell swords and buy them again are aiding in the plowing of rain and clouds; those who wield knives and cut are opening the way for theft and burglary,' where '卖剑而买之者' refers to those who engage in illegal activities, and '鼓刀而割之者' refers to those who commit acts of violence.

  • 弗顾邑灾，惟恃庖丁善解；竟同蹊夺，何论犁子为騂 - Means 'they do not care about the disaster in the county, relying only on the butcher's skill; they act like thieves, how can they distinguish between oxen and horses,' where '庖丁' refers to a skilled butcher, and '蹊夺' refers to acting like a thief.

  • 即庄人遇群狼 - Means 'when villagers encountered a pack of wolves,' setting the scene.
  • 内有短尾者 - Means 'among them was one with a short tail,' identifying the wolf.
  • 以如意呼之 - Means 'calling it Rúyì,' describing the villagers' action.
  • 辄率群狼而去 - Means 'it would lead the pack away,' describing the wolf's response.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狼 - Wolves are often depicted in Chinese folklore as dangerous and cunning creatures.
  • 如意 - A term that can mean 'as one wishes' or 'favorable,' but in this context, it seems to refer to a specific wolf with a unique characteristic (lack of a tail).
  • 庄人 - Refers to villagers or local people, indicating a rural setting.
  • 群狼 - A pack of wolves, emphasizing the danger and collective threat.
  • 短尾者 - A wolf with a short tail, a distinctive feature that sets it apart from others.

Proper Nouns

  • 才 - A name, likely the main character of the story.
  • 如意 - The name of the wolf that helps the main character.

  • 见狼汗出如洗 - Literal translation: 'seeing the wolf sweating profusely.'

  • 后辄夜间衔物来 - Literal translation: 'later, they would come at night bringing objects.'
  • 才以小康 - Literal translation: 'the talent lived in relative comfort.'
  • 好事者每以饭食之 - Literal translation: 'those who were fond of it often fed it with food.'

  • 人屡见如意卧墓旁 - Literal translation: 'people often saw 'Like Intention' lying by the grave.'
  • 年馀始不见 - Literal translation: 'after more than a year, it was no longer seen.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 白金 - Refers to silver, which was highly valued in ancient China.

  • 庄外 - Refers to the outskirts of a village or settlement, indicating a rural setting.

Proper Nouns

  • 辄有以报之 - Means 'there is always a way to repay it,' indicating the possibility or duty of returning kindness.

  • 人面狼心者，吾闻其语矣 - Means 'I have heard of people with wolf hearts but human faces,' referring to hypocrites who appear virtuous but act maliciously.
  • 狼面人心者，未见其人也 - Means 'I have not seen anyone with a wolf's face but a human heart,' suggesting the rarity of truly virtuous individuals.
  • 施报分明 - Means 'clear distinctions between giving and receiving,' emphasizing fairness in reciprocity.
  • 盖防如豺字从才，狼字从良 - Cryptic phrase likely referring to the etymology of the characters '豺' (jackal) and '狼' (wolf), suggesting that these animals possess some qualities of '才' (talent) and '良' (goodness), respectively.
  • 宋王懿之白狼童子 - Refers to a legendary figure in Chinese folklore, a white wolf child associated with the Song Dynasty ruler Wang Yi.

  • 姚五官 - A proper name, likely referring to a person in the story.

  • 可足当年吃著 - Means 'just enough for the current year's needs,' emphasizing a simple, self-sufficient lifestyle.
  • 甫二岁而姚卒 - Means 'just two years old when Yao died,' indicating a young age at the time of death.
  • 羊喻利之妹 - Refers to 'Yang Yuli's sister,' a character in the story.
  • 择市沃田 - Means 'selecting fertile fields near the market,' suggesting a strategic choice of land.
  • 阴将姚产粮籍悉改拨己名 - Means 'secretly changing Yao's property records to her own name,' indicating dishonest or deceitful behavior.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有富室张某 - Refers to a wealthy man named Zhang, who is the main character of the story.
  • 爱五官慧丽 - Means 'loves the five senses of beauty,' referring to the physical attractiveness of a woman.
  • 欲购作妾 - Means 'wants to buy her as a concubine,' indicating Zhang's intention to acquire the woman as a secondary wife.
  • 遂诣羊所，直言其事而许以白金二百两 - Means 'then went to Yang's place, directly stated his intention and offered two hundred taels of silver,' describing Zhang's approach to Yang.
  • 羊大喜，遂将五官诳至其家，强付张从人舁去 - Means 'Yang was very happy, then tricked the woman to his home and forcibly handed her over to Zhang's people who carried her away,' detailing Yang's actions.
  • 其银则羊吞使过半，氏未得蓰之一焉 - Means 'the silver was mostly taken by Yang, and the woman did not receive even a fraction of it,' highlighting Yang's greed.
  • 嗣逼氏醮 - Means 'later, Yang forced the woman to divorce her husband,' describing Yang's next action.
  • 氏以夫死未久，且以有三岁幼子，不忍委弃，矢不改嫁 - Means 'the woman, whose husband had recently died, and who had a three-year-old son, could not bear to abandon them, and vowed not to remarry,' explaining the woman's refusal.
  • 羊言之再四，氏终不从 - Means 'Yang spoke to her repeatedly, but the woman still refused,' showing the persistence of both parties.
  • 羊邻庄有棍徒李某，闻羊妹美而寡，烦羊媒之，啖以重酬 - Means 'Yang's neighbor, a ruffian named Li, heard that Yang's sister was beautiful and widowed, and asked Yang to act as a matchmaker, offering a large reward,' introducing Li's involvement.
  • 羊亦不与妹谋，令李强夺之以去 - Means 'Yang did not consult his sister, and ordered Li to forcibly take her away,' showing Yang's manipulation.
  • 氏至李家，矢不与李同寝，百词劝解，氏卒不听 - Means 'the woman went to Li's house, vowed not to sleep with him, and despite many attempts to persuade her, she refused to comply,' describing the woman's resistance.
  • 李怒，赤其身而鞭之，上下几无完肤，滨于死矣 - Means 'Li became angry, stripped her naked and beat her, leaving her almost dead,' detailing the violence.
  • 比伤愈，复欲犯之，氏仍不从 - Means 'after she recovered, Li again wanted to violate her, but the woman still refused,' showing the ongoing conflict.
  • 氏曰：“若然，吾以原金自赎可乎？” - Means 'the woman said, 'If so, may I redeem myself with the original money?'' proposing a solution.
  • 李曰：“可。” - Means 'Li said, 'Yes,' agreeing to the proposal.
  • 氏所得五官身价犹在，遂举以授李，李乃听氏自去 - Means 'the woman still had the money she received from Zhang, so she gave it to Li, and Li allowed her to leave,' resolving the situation.
  • 氏欲依兄，恐复逼令改节，遂栖止孤贫院后，佣于富室作针黹，而去家少远矣 - Means 'the woman wanted to stay with her brother, but feared he would again force her to change her status, so she stayed in a poor house behind a temple, worked for a wealthy household doing needlework, and lived far from home,' describing her final actions.
  • 羊氏所生之子，姚在时命名曰增，羊视如犬豕 - Means 'Yang's son, whom Yao named Zeng when he was alive, was treated by Yang like a dog or pig,' showing Yang's mistreatment of his son.
  • 年方五岁，即使丐食于乡，见之者无不恨羊之无良也 - Means 'at five years old, Yang forced him to beg in the village, and those who saw him all hated Yang for his wickedness,' detailing the son's suffering and public reaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 富室 - Refers to a wealthy household, reflecting the social hierarchy of the time.
  • 妾 - Refers to a concubine, indicating the practice of polygamy and the status of women in the society.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, a valuable currency in the period.
  • 媒 - Refers to a matchmaker, reflecting the role of intermediaries in marriage arrangements.
  • 孤贫院 - Refers to a temple or shelter for the poor and destitute, showing the existence of such institutions.
  • 针黹 - Refers to needlework, indicating the type of work women did in the household.

Proper Nouns

  • 张某 - The name of the wealthy man Zhang.
  • 羊 - The name of Yang, the woman's maternal uncle.
  • 五官 - The name of the woman.
  • 李某 - The name of Li, the ruffian.
  • 增 - The name of Yang's son, given by Yao.
  • 姚 - The name of Yao, the deceased husband of the woman.

  • 五官见羊辄詈 - 五官 is the name of the person, 见羊 means to see Yang, 辄 means always, and 詈 means to curse.

  • 忽忆甥在孟氏 - 忽忆 means suddenly remember, 甥 means nephew, and 在孟氏 means in the Shang family.

  • 遂托异姓女欲醮 - 遂 means then, 托 means to entrust, 异姓女 means a woman from another family, and 欲醮 means to want to marry her.

  • 及过门 - 及 means when, and 过门 means to pass through the door (marry into the family).

  • 五官见房舍系母家故物 - 五官 is the name of the person, 见 means to see, 房舍 means house, 系 means belonging to, 母家 means mother's family, and 故物 means old things.
  • 遂谓增曰 - 遂 means then, 谓 means to say, and 增 is the name of the brother.

  • 然 - 然 means yes.
  • 吾系汝之胞姐 - 吾 means I, 系 means am, 汝 means you, 之 means of, and 胞姐 means biological sister.
  • 以姊弟作夫妇，行同禽兽矣 - 以 means to, 姊弟 means sister and brother, 作夫妇 means to marry, 行 means behavior, 同 means the same as, 禽兽 means beasts, and 矣 means particle indicating completed action.
  • 遂令增各寝 - 遂 means then, 令 means to order, 增 is the name of the brother, and 各寝 means to sleep separately.
  • 次日，五官令增将羊未字之女诳至 - 次日 means the next day, 五官 is the name of the person, 令 means to order, 增 is the name of the brother, 将 means to bring, 羊 is the name of the person, 未字之女 means the unmarried daughter, and 诳至 means to deceive her to come.
  • 强令与增合卺 - 强令 means to force, 与 means and, 增 is the name of the brother, and 合卺 means to marry.

Cultural and Historical Allusions
